commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0fedcf90f6de80e75d1c8ad2e8bf9109a50f7379)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Mattermost thread fetch helper, backfills empty in-memory thread history before inbound context construction, gates backfills by a stored session id, and adds client/monitor tests.

PR surface: Source +143, Tests +385. Total +528 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main keeps Mattermost thread history in a process-local map and records thread turns under ctxPayload.SessionKey, while the PR's reset gate can be checked against the thread-scoped session key path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code defect is narrow, but this external PR also needs refreshed contributor proof after repair, so it should stay human-handled rather than queued for ClawSweeper repair.

target.title: fix(mattermost): backfill thread history from server when in-memory window is empty
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Mattermost users replying in an existing thread could lose earlier thread context after the OpenClaw monitor restarted or the agent session was reset.

Fixes #93204

## Why This Change Was Made

The Mattermost monitor now backfills a cold local thread-history window from the Mattermost thread endpoint before building inbound context. The backfill marker is keyed by the stored agent `sessionId`, not the stable route session key, so a real `/new` or session reset gets its own recovery fetch while same-session follow-ups avoid repeated server fetches.

## User Impact

Mattermost thread replies keep useful prior context after restart or `/new`, without adding repeated thread-history requests to normal same-session follow-ups.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts`: 72 passed; covers stable `route.sessionKey`, stored `sessionId` rotation, same-session no-refetch, and reset-session refetch.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`: passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.

Live proof: local Docker Mattermost Team Edition 10.11.21 at `http://localhost:8065`, with tokens and Mattermost ids redacted. The proof used real Mattermost REST auth, posts, and `/api/v4/posts/<root>/thread` through `monitorMattermostProvider`; a fake websocket only injected `posted` events into the monitor. The route/thread session key stayed stable while the stored agent `sessionId` rotated. Cold start included prior root/thread context, same-session server-only context stayed absent, and stored-session reset included new server-only prior context:

```json
{
  "mattermost": "10.11.21 local Docker, token/id redacted",
  "routeSessionKey": "stable, redacted",
  "threadSessionKeyStable": true,
  "storedSessionIds": [
    "stored-session-before-reset",
    "stored-session-after-reset"
  ],
  "dispatchCount": 3,
  "firstColdStartContainsPriorThreadContext": true,
  "firstColdStartContainsRootContext": true,
  "sameSessionSkippedServerOnlyContext": true,
  "storedSessionIdResetContainsNewPriorContext": true
}
```

AI-assisted: built with Codex
target.author: sunlit-deng

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
